title: Pinterest V2 Day 1 — Image Review
status: AWAITING_APPROVAL — Do not post until reviewed

pins.hook: $100K Income: How Much House Can You Afford?
pins.title: How Much House Can You Afford on $100K Income? — Free Calculator
pins.description: On a $100,000 annual income, you can typically afford a home between $300,000 and $400,000 depending on your down payment, debts, and interest rate. This free calculator factors in taxes, insurance, PMI, and HOA fees to give you your exact number — not a rough estimate.
pins.score_notes: Clean white background, giant $300K-$400K number in teal on a white card with subtle shadow. 52px bold headline readable on mobile. CTA button 'Calculate Your Exact Number' is specific and personal. Only improvement: could use slightly more visual depth on the card.

pins.hook: Debt Snowball vs Avalanche: Which Pays Off Faster?
pins.title: Debt Snowball vs Avalanche Method — Which Pays Off Faster? Free Comparison
pins.description: The avalanche method saves $2,347 more in interest on a $30K debt, but the snowball method pays off the first debt 8 months sooner. Which is right for you? Compare both strategies side by side with your exact debts — free, instant results.
pins.score_notes: Side-by-side cards with clear ★ winner badge on Avalanche. Green victory bar spans full width. Specific dollar amount ($2,347) and time (8 months) make it concrete. Two cards at 410px each may feel tight on phone but still readable.

pins.hook: Before You Buy a Home, Check These 7 Numbers
pins.title: 7 Numbers to Check Before Buying a Home — Free Calculator Checklist
pins.description: Before you start house hunting, calculate these 7 numbers: 1) Credit score, 2) Debt-to-income ratio, 3) Down payment needed, 4) Monthly payment estimate, 5) Closing costs, 6) Emergency fund after purchase, 7) Total cost over 30 years. Use our free calculators for all 7 — instant results.
pins.score_notes: Warm cream background (#FFFBEB) feels approachable. 7 green numbered circles with specific benchmarks (620+ credit, 36% DTI). Clean vertical stack, good spacing. Most saveable format — checklists are Pinterest's top-performing pin type. Destination is mortgage-affordability which only covers some checklist items directly — minor mismatch.

pins.hook: 5 Mortgage Mistakes That Make a Home Unaffordable
pins.title: 5 Mortgage Mistakes That Make Your Home Unaffordable — Avoid These
pins.description: Most homebuyers make at least one of these costly mistakes: 1) Maxing out your budget, 2) Skipping rate shopping, 3) Ignoring PMI costs, 4) Forgetting closing costs, 5) Not checking your credit first. Use our free mortgage calculator to see your actual affordable number.
pins.score_notes: Light red tinted background creates urgency without fear. Red left borders on mistake cards. Specific dollar impacts ($30K+, $100-300/month) make warnings concrete. Lower visual score due to denser text — 5 mistakes with dollar impacts is content-heavy for a pin. CTA 'Check Your Affordable Number' specific.
issues: Text density slightly high — consider reducing to 4 mistakes for cleaner layout

pins.hook: $400K Home at 6.5%: What's the Monthly Payment?
pins.title: $400,000 Home at 6.5% Interest — Your Monthly Payment Breakdown
pins.description: A $400,000 home with 20% down at 6.5% APR equals approximately $2,022/month principal & interest. With taxes, insurance, and PMI, expect $2,500-2,800/month total. See your exact payment for any home price, rate, or down payment — free instant calculator.
pins.score_notes: Strongest visual — realistic calculator result card with Inputs → Result flow. 3 input rows, divider, giant $2,022 in teal. Alternative scenario tease (FHA at 3.5%) adds curiosity value. Clean white card on light gray background. Highest overall quality score.
